what_went_well: Thorough analysis of both bash source files (foundry-batch.sh ~298 lines, foundry-common.sh ~1695 lines) to identify exact functions to migrate
Identified all integration points with existing TS modules (task-state-v2.ts, runner.ts, executor.ts, git.ts, events.ts)
Design leverages async workers instead of child processes — simpler architecture that matches Node.js strengths
File locking design uses O_CREAT|O_EXCL which is atomic on Linux/macOS without external dependencies
Spec covers 25+ scenarios with Given/When/Then format covering all critical paths
improvement_suggestions: Consider adding a feature flag (FOUNDRY_TS_BATCH=true) for gradual rollout if the team wants safer migration
The spec doesn't cover Windows compatibility — if cross-platform is needed, file locking strategy may need adjustment
Integration tests with actual git worktrees would be valuable but are hard to automate in CI
deviations_from_spec: agentic-development has no pre-existing openspec/ directory — created it fresh for this proposal
